@@ -0,0 +1,75 @@
+{
+  "version": "kc-mission-v1",
+  "name": "grpc-4629-gevent-compatibility",
+  "missionClass": "solution",
+  "author": "KubeStellar Bot",
+  "authorGithub": "kubestellar",
+  "mission": {
+    "title": "grpc: gevent compatibility",
+    "description": "gevent compatibility. Requested by 19+ users.",
+    "type": "feature",
+    "status": "completed",
+    "steps": [
+      {
+        "title": "Check current grpc setup",
+        "description": "Verify your grpc version:\ngRPC library — check your language-specific package (e.g., pip show grpcio, npm ls @grpc/grpc-js)\nThis feature requires grpc as a dependency."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Review grpc configuration",
+        "description": "Review the relevant grpc configuration:\nHi, I want to make grpc client and server code to work with gevent, is grpc compatible with gevent?"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Apply the fix for gevent compatibility",
+        "description": "It's basically that. gRPC Python uses multiple threads for a server (e.g. one for the server receiving messages, one to pass the call data along to the application). These trip over each other when they don't cooperatively release polling to `gevent` (which at least one of them can't do because\n```yaml\nFile \"/Users/srinath.gs/.virtualenvs/vajra/lib/python2.7/site-packages/grpc/_channel.py\", line 670, in channel_spin\n    event = state.completion_queue.poll()\n  File \"src/python/grpcio/grpc/_cython/_cygrpc/completion_queue.pyx.pxi\", line 105, in grpc._cython.cygrpc.CompletionQueue.poll (src/python/grpcio/grpc/_cython/cygrpc.c:9612)\nKeyboardInterrupt\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Verify the feature works",
+        "description": "Test that the new capability is working as expected.\nConfirm the feature described in \"gevent compatibility\" is functioning correctly."
+      }
+    ],
+    "resolution": {
+      "summary": "It's basically that. gRPC Python uses multiple threads for a server (e.g. one for the server receiving messages, one to pass the call data along to the application). These trip over each other when they don't cooperatively release polling to `gevent` (which at least one of them can't do because it's in gRPC core code and the core is obviously not based on `gevent`).",
+      "codeSnippets": [
+        "File \"/Users/srinath.gs/.virtualenvs/vajra/lib/python2.7/site-packages/grpc/_channel.py\", line 670, in channel_spin\n    event = state.completion_queue.poll()\n  File \"src/python/grpcio/grpc/_cython/_cygrpc/completion_queue.pyx.pxi\", line 105, in grpc._cython.cygrpc.CompletionQueue.poll (src/python/grpcio/grpc/_cython/cygrpc.c:9612)\nKeyboardInterrupt",
+        "rpc Read(ReadFileRequest) returns (stream ReadFileResponse);",
+        "def read(self, chunk=1024):\n        rfreq = file_pb2.ReadFileRequest(file=self.f, chunk=chunk)\n        for resp in self.stub.Read(rfreq, self.timeout):\n            print resp"
+      ]
+    }
+  },
